title: Which emerging battery chemistries ship before 2030?

messages.content: Give me a structured brief on emerging battery chemistries expected to reach commercial shipments before 2030. Focus on energy density, cycle life, and supply-chain risk.

Here is the brief. Three chemistries dominate the near-term pipeline:

1. Solid-state lithium-metal — energy density ~450 Wh/kg, cycle life > 1000 at lab scale; Toyota and QuantumScape lead. Supply-chain risk: lithium carbonate and dry-room capacity.
2. Sodium-ion — lower density (~160 Wh/kg) but sodium abundance and better cold-weather performance; CATL shipping first-gen cells for BESS use today.
3. Lithium-sulfur — high theoretical density (~500 Wh/kg) but cycle life stuck below 500 in most literature; Oxis Energy restructured, Lyten is the current front-runner.

Open questions: gigafactory capex curve after 2027, reuse/recycle economics for Na-ion.

metadata.reasoning_summary: Drafted the three near-term chemistries, ranked by 2030 shipment likelihood. Pulled cycle-life figures from manufacturer briefings + preprints.
